M-038.NAME: compact-ui-system
M-038.owns: Shared compact UI rules for user and admin frontends: mobile-first layout, dense information surfaces, simple navigation, and readable controls

exports.rule-mobile-layout: Responsive layout rules for phone-first screens without horizontal scroll
exports.rule-compact-controls: Standard sizes and interaction patterns for buttons, lists, tabs, dialogs, and confirmations
exports.rule-content-priority: Priority order for visible information on user and admin mobile surfaces
exports.rule-visual-density: Reduced spacing, restrained panels, and non-decorative backgrounds for operational screens
exports.rule-safe-text: Wrapping, truncation, and stable sizing rules for long emails, device keys, status labels, and payment text

rules.rule-1: Operational pages start with the usable product surface, not a marketing hero or explanatory landing section.
rules.rule-2: Cards must not be nested inside other cards; page sections should be flat, dense, and scan-friendly.
rules.rule-3: Use compact list rows, segmented controls, tabs, icon buttons, and concise labels instead of large decorative card grids.
rules.rule-4: Phone navigation must keep primary actions reachable without a 320px desktop sidebar or repeated identity/status panels.
rules.rule-5: Text must not overlap adjacent content; critical values must wrap or truncate intentionally within stable containers.
rules.rule-6: Prefer lucide icons in action buttons where an icon exists; do not replace familiar icons with text-only controls.
rules.rule-7: Reduce large border radii and glass styling during execution; target compact operational panels rather than oversized rounded sections.
rules.rule-8: Do not use decorative radial-gradient/orb backgrounds as the visual basis for the cabinet or admin work surfaces.
rules.rule-9: Destructive or disruptive admin actions must use confirmation-safe mobile affordances instead of immediate table-row mutations.
rules.rule-10: Charts and secondary analytics panels must collapse behind concise KPI summaries on phone-first admin surfaces.

phase-boundaries.phase-23: User frontend adopts compact UI rules for the personal cabinet.
phase-boundaries.phase-24: Admin frontend adopted the same compact UI rules for operator overview, users, devices, payments, analytics, and system health workflows.
phase-boundaries.phase-25: Session security changes are separate and must not be bundled into UI simplification.

constraints.constraint-1: No new marketing-style landing pages for operational surfaces.
constraints.constraint-2: Use compact lists, segmented views, and clear action buttons instead of heavy decorative card grids.
constraints.constraint-3: Text must fit on mobile without overlap, truncation of critical values, or horizontal scroll.
constraints.constraint-4: Do not touch deploy/install scripts while applying UI rules.
constraints.constraint-5: Do not mix session token hardening into compact UI execution; Phase-25 owns that risk.

verification.check-2: No text overlap in nav, buttons, rows, modals, or status areas.
verification.check-3: Primary action buttons have stable dimensions across loading, empty, success, and error states.
verification.check-4: Admin device confirmations stay usable at 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932.

execution-notes.note-1: Phase-23 user frontend compact UI passed local build and mobile smoke.
execution-notes.note-2: Phase-24 admin frontend compact UI passed local build and Chrome/CDP mobile smoke.
execution-notes.note-3: M-038 is done locally for the planned user/admin compact UI scope; Phase-25 session security remains separate.
